title: mentha arvensis subsp. gentilis
intro: Mentha arvensis subsp. gentilis, commonly known as wild mint, is a subspecies of the mint plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its tall, slender stems and small, green leaves with purple flowers. It is renowned for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds like menthol, menthone, and limonene, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of wild mint include alleviating digestive issues, relieving stress and anxiety, and providing relief from headaches and fever. Wild mint can be consumed in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in cooking and traditional medicine.
